title: godetia tenella
intro: Godetia tenella, also known as the lesser godetia, is a flowering plant species in the family Polemoniaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a low-growing, annual or biennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been reported to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. Godetia tenella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its flowers can also be used to make a soothing salve or poultice for topical application.
